key_evidence: India accused of forcing 40 Rohingya refugees (incl. minors, elderly, ill) off navy vessel into Andaman Sea — UN special investigator assigned
Refoulement of UNHCR-registered refugees = violation of 1951 Convention and customary international law
India has not confirmed nor denied — factual record relies on Rohingya testimony + UN inquiry pending
HRW ILO gig worker report (May 13) documents Indian platform-worker conditions
Boundary-proximity (22.7 vs 20.0 Critical/Developing boundary): standalone apply would force band crossing
methodology_note: BOUNDARY-WATCH SUB-THRESHOLD ACCUMULATOR (Tier C). Rohingya refoulement event recorded in evidence; apply held pending UN investigator findings (firms factual base) or additional categorically-novel accumulation. Band-crossing protocol requires founder decision for any movement that would cross 20.0 boundary.
